name: the-color-of-money
description: Mehrsa Baradaran's 'The Color of Money: Black Banks and the Racial Wealth Gap' — a history showing how Black banks were promoted as a solution to inequality but could never succeed because they were asked to fix a problem created by systemic discrimination.
useCase: Understand the racial wealth gap, the history of Black banking, how government policy created inequality, and what actually works to close the gap.
knownRisks: Academic work — dense reading, focused on US history
Strongly argued thesis — Baradaran is critical of self-help and entrepreneurship solutions
May challenge readers who believe in colorblind economics or bootstrap narratives
